Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Plaintiff'S Attorney
Off Calendar Mar-22-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Joseph Schoepp's motion for terminating and monetary sanctions is granted. No opposition has been filed. The Court finds that further orders would not result in compliance. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Plaintiff'S Attorney
Off Calendar Feb-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant joseph Schoepp's motion for terminating and monetary sanctions is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005, requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. The motion was filed on January 19, 2007. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set Two) And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Deft'S Motion To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jan-24-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant JOSEPH SCHOEPP'S motion to compel responses to request for production set #2 is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objection and to produce documents within 21 days of notice of this order. Failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $490 are to be paid within 21 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Judicial Council Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-31-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories is Granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff is ordered to provide further verified responses and responsive documents within 15 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that plaintiff failed to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the matters in dispute. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $580 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2006 Off Calendar

Re-Notice Of Mo For Protective Order As To Deposition Subpoena To Tyson Fire Investigations, Inc
Off Calendar May-01-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mark R. Mittelman for defendant and moving party Joseph Schoepp
Karen Hull for plaintiff and opposing party Jose Pacab

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Joseph Shoepp's motion for a protective order is granted.
The Court has read and considered the late filed opposition. As drafted, the subpoena is overbroad. The parties shall make a reasonable and good faith attempt to informally resolve the issues in dispute by modifying the current subpoena. Counsel for plaintiff is ordered to comply with the requirements of CCP Section 1005 as to any future motions. The parties shall also comply with the requirements of California Rule of Court 313(d). The request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
